changes.name: Skill: Setup Cowork role selection
changes.sample:
## Step 1 — Role

Your initial message should frame what Cowork is: it autonomously handles tasks like reading your email, searching your docs, drafting reports, etc. Educate the user on _Skills_, reu

changes.name: Agent Prompt: Background job agent instructions
changes.sample: This session is a background job. The user may be live or away — respond naturally either way. A classifier reads only your message text (not tool output, subagent reports, or human replies) to track

changes.name: Skill: Code Review (--fix applying fixes)
changes.sample:
## Applying fixes (--fix)

The \`--fix\` flag was passed. After producing the findings list, apply the
findings to the working tree instead of stopping at the report: fix each one
directly — correct

changes.name: Skill: Code Review (high effort)
changes.sample:
\`high effort → 3+5 angles × 6 candidates → 1-vote verify (recall-biased) → ≤10 findings\`

You are reviewing for **recall** at high effort: catch every real bug a careful
reviewer would catch in one

changes.name: Skill: Code Review (max / xhigh effort)
changes.sample:
\`${} effort → 5+5 angles × 8 candidates → 1-vote verify → sweep → ≤15 findings\`

You are reviewing for **recall** at ${==="max"?"maximum":"extra-high"} effort: catch every real bug. At
this level, c

changes.name: Skill: Code Review (medium effort)
changes.sample:
\`medium effort → 3+5 angles × 6 candidates → 1-vote verify → ≤8 findings\`

You are reviewing for **precision** at medium effort: every finding you surface
should be one a maintainer would act on.

$
